REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT JEROLD GRISEZ'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF SHALL ALLEGE FACTS, NOT CONCLUSIONS, SHOWING THAT MR. GRISEZ IS AN "OWNER" WITHIN THE MEANING OF GOVT. CODE 12927. PLAINTIFF SHALL FURTHER ALLEGE FACTS, IN GOOD FAITH AND IN LIGHT OF CORP. CODE 17703, SHOWING THAT MR. GRISEZ IS PERSONALLY LIABLE. THE COURT NOTES THAT OPPOSITION FAILED TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT GOVERNMENT CODE 12927 TRUMPS THE PROTECTIONS AFFORDED BY CORP. CODE TO MEMBERS OF LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANIES. IF PLAINTIFF INTENDS TO PURSUE THIS ACTION BASING LIABILITY ON THIS THEORY PLAINTIFF SHALL ONLY DO SO IN GOOD FAITH. PLAINTIFFS INFORMS THE COURT THAT THE STATUTORY TEN DAYS TO AMEND FALLS IN SUNDAY OCTOBER 28, 2018 AND REQUESTS UNTIL OCTOBER 29, 2018 TO FILE THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE DEFENDANT DOES NOT OBJECT. Renotice Of Hearing On Motion For Monetary And Nonmonetary Expenses And Sanctions Including Attorneys Fees Against Plaintiff Pursuant To Ccp 128.7
Off Calendar Sep-26-2019 Continued To Oct-03-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT GRISEZ BUCHANAN, LLC, JEROLD GRISEZ Renotice Of Hearing On Motion For Monetary And Nonmonetary Expenses And Sanctions Including Attorneys Fees Against Plaintiff Pursuant To Ccp 128.7 is continued to October 3, 2019. By no later than September 27, 2019 Plaintiff to provide a supplemental declaration addressing the questions listed below. Do no provide any additional argument if the requested responses is "Yes or No."

1. Is an existing and current as of the date of the filing (as opposed to May 2015) determination of disability/need for accommodation (here, prescribed emotional support animal) a necessary element of any of the alleged in the Complaint and First Amended Complaint.?
Yes or No.
2. Is an existing and current (as opposed to May 2015) determination of disability/need for accommodation (here, prescribed emotional support animal) a necessary element of request for injunction sought by Plaintiff?
Yes or No.
3. Is evidence of existing and current (as opposed to May 2015) determination of disability/need for accommodation (here, prescribed emotional support animal) a necessary element of any of the claims (including relief sought, such as injunction) alleged in the Complaint and First Amended Complaint.
Yes or No
4. Is an existing and current (as opposed to May 2015) determination of disability/need for accommodation (here, prescribed emotional support animal) a factor considered by DFEH in filing and maintaining an action against a landlord.
Yes or No.
5. Is evidence of existing and current (as opposed to May 2015) determination of disability/need for accommodation (here, prescribed emotional support animal) a factor considered by DFEH in filing and maintaining an action against a landlord.
Yes or No.
6. May DFEH maintain an action for discrimination and civil rights violation (including injunction) without evidence of existing disability requiring accommodation during the pendency of litigation?
Yes or No.

If answer to all the questions above is "No," the following questions do not require responses. If any question above is answered in the affirmative, please provide responses to the following questions:
1. List (and attach) all the medical evidence of existing and current disability (re: support animal) relied upon at the time of the filing of the Complaint.
2. List (and attach) all the medical evidence of existing and current disability (re: support animal) relied upon at the time of the filing of the First Amended Complaint.

Defendant may file a response declaration addressing the same questions by no later than October 1, 2019. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
